Sends payment. Calls a paid endpoint on an onboarded APIHub service. Debits the endpoint's price from your credit balance and forwards the request to the upstream provider. Returns an object with the upstream response body, HTTP status, and credits_charged_microdollars. Requires a valid API key and

labels.name: apihub_search_external
labels.description: Search external x402-protected APIs (not operated by APIHub, but callable via credits). Returns listings with endpoint counts, prices, and on-chain activity.

labels.name: apihub_call_external
labels.description: Call an external x402-protected URL (any provider in the marketplace or any x402 API). APIHub pays the provider on your behalf using the platform wallet and debits your credit balance for the exact amount. No wallet or gas required.

labels.name: apihub_read_content
labels.description: Read web content through a paid content gateway. Returns clean, structured text extracted from the URL. Use this for content services (service_type = 'content').

labels.name: apihub_topup
labels.description: Purchase APIHub credits via x402 (USDC on Base). Returns payment instructions including a web URL for browser-based payment, a CLI command, and raw x402 requirements for agents with wallet support. Credits are added to your account instantly once payment confirms on-chain.

labels.name: apihub_balance
labels.description: Read-only. Returns your current APIHub credit balance (in microdollars and USD), total lifetime spending (microdollars and USD), and total completed request count. Requires a valid API key. Use before apihub_call or apihub_call_external to confirm sufficient funds for a paid request, or periodically

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: apihub_call, apihub_read_content · Private data: apihub_call, apihub_call_external, apihub_topup, apihub_balance · Public sink: apihub_balance · Destructive: apihub_balance.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: apihub_call, apihub_read_content · Private data: apihub_call, apihub_call_external, apihub_topup, apihub_balance · Public sink: apihub_balance · Destructive: apihub_balance

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
